title: Idiyappam

ingredients.name: rice flour (arisi maavu), fine-milled
visual_checks: use idiyappam rice flour or fine-ground rice flour, not coarse
ingredients.notes: use idiyappam rice flour or fine-ground rice flour, not coarse

subs.name: store-bought idiyappam instant flour mix
subs.notes: pre-salted; no dry-roasting needed; follow package water ratio

ingredients.name: hot water
visual_checks: just off the boil; hot water makes the dough smooth and pliable
ingredients.notes: just off the boil; hot water makes the dough smooth and pliable

ingredients.name: oil
visual_checks: coconut oil preferred; mixed into dough
ingredients.notes: coconut oil preferred; mixed into dough

subs.name: coconut-milk (100 ml, sweetened with sugar and cardamom)
subs.notes: pour over the idiyappam as a sauce rather than garnish

steps.text: Dry-roast the rice flour in a dry pan on medium-low flame, stirring, for 2 minutes until it is warm to the touch and smells faintly of cooked rice. This pre-toasting makes the dough easier to press. Tip into a mixing bowl.

Add salt and oil to the warm flour. Gradually pour in the very hot water while mixing with a spoon, adding just enough to bring the flour together. When it is cool enough to handle, knead it briefly for 1 minute into a soft, smooth, non-sticky dough. It should feel like soft Play-Doh.
Fill the idiyappam press (or a sev/murukku press fitted with the fine-hole disc) with the dough. Grease the idli plates or a steamer basket with a few drops of oil.

Press the dough through the mold in a circular, overlapping motion over each idli cup or plate, building up a loose, cloud-like nest about 2 cm thick. Do not press too densely — the strands should be light and tangled, not compacted.

Pour water into the base of the idli steamer, bring to a boil, then place the idiyappam nests inside. Cover and steam on medium-high flame for 8–10 minutes until the nests are cooked through — they will look translucent and feel firm but not dry.

Remove carefully with a spoon and place on serving plates. Scatter grated coconut on top. Serve immediately with coconut milk sweetened with a pinch of sugar and cardamom, or with vegetable stew or chicken kulambu.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: rice-flour → store-bought idiyappam instant flour mix (pre-salted; no dry-roasting needed; follow package water ratio); coconut → coconut-milk (100 ml, sweetened with sugar and cardamom) (pour over the idiyappam as a sauce rather than garnish)
